00020  * ARE DISCLAIMED.  IN NO EVENT SHALL THE UNIVERSITY OR CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE
00021  * FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL
00022  * DAMAGES (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS
00023  * OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION)
00024  * HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, STRICT

00026  * OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF
00027  * SUCH DAMAGE.

00029 
00030 /*
00031  * fstest - filesystem test code
00032  *
00033  * Writes a file (in small chunks) and then reads it back again
00034  * (also in small chunks) and complains if what it reads back is
00035  * not the same.
00036  *
00037  * The length of SLOGAN is intentionally a prime number and 
00038  * specifically *not* a power of two.
00039  */
00040 
00041 #include <types.h>
00042 #include <kern/errno.h>
00043 #include <kern/fcntl.h>
00044 #include <lib.h>
00045 #include <uio.h>
00046 #include <thread.h>
00047 #include <synch.h>
00048 #include <vfs.h>
00049 #include <fs.h>
00050 #include <vnode.h>
00051 #include <test.h>
00052 
00053 #define SLOGAN   "HODIE MIHI - CRAS TIBI\n"
00054 #define FILENAME "fstest.tmp"
00055 #define NCHUNKS  720
00056 #define NTHREADS 12
00057 #define NCREATES 32
00058 
00059 static struct semaphore *threadsem = NULL;
00060 
00061 static
00062 void
00063 init_threadsem(void)
00064 {
00065         if (threadsem==NULL) {
00066                 threadsem = sem_create("fstestsem", 0);
00067                 if (threadsem == NULL) {
00068                         panic("fstest: sem_create failed\n");
00069                 }
00070         }
00071 }
00072 
00073 /*
00074  * Vary each line of the test file in a way that's predictable but
00075  * unlikely to mask bugs in the filesystem.
00076  */
00077 static
00078 void
00079 rotate(char *str, int amt)
00080 {
00081         int i, ch;
00082 
00083         amt = (amt+2600)%26;
00084         KASSERT(amt>=0);
00085 
00086         for (i=0; str[i]; i++) {
00087                 ch = str[i];
00088                 if (ch>='A' && ch<='Z') {
00089                         ch = ch - 'A';
00090                         ch += amt;
00091                         ch %= 26;
00092                         ch = ch + 'A';
00093                         KASSERT(ch>='A' && ch<='Z');
00094                 }
00095                 str[i] = ch;
00096         }
00097 }
00098 
00100 
00101 static
00102 void
00103 fstest_makename(char *buf, size_t buflen, 
00104                 const char *fs, const char *namesuffix)
00105 {
00106         snprintf(buf, buflen, "%s:%s%s", fs, FILENAME, namesuffix);
00107         KASSERT(strlen(buf) < buflen);
00108 }
00109 
00110 #define MAKENAME() fstest_makename(name, sizeof(name), fs, namesuffix)
00111 
00112 static
00113 int
00114 fstest_remove(const char *fs, const char *namesuffix)
00115 {
00116         char name[32];
00117         char buf[32];
00118         int err;
00119 
00120         MAKENAME();
00121 
00122         strcpy(buf, name);
00123         err = vfs_remove(buf);
00124         if (err) {
00125                 kprintf("Could not remove %s: %s\n", name, strerror(err));
00126                 return -1;
00127         }
00128         
00129         return 0;
00130 }
00131 
00132 static
00133 int
00134 fstest_write(const char *fs, const char *namesuffix,
00135              int stridesize, int stridepos)
00136 {
00137         struct vnode *vn;
00138         int err;
00139         int i;
00140         size_t shouldbytes=0;
00141         size_t bytes=0;
00142         off_t pos=0;
00143         char name[32];
00144         char buf[32];
00145         struct iovec iov;
00146         struct uio ku;
00147         int flags;
00148 
00149         KASSERT(sizeof(buf) > strlen(SLOGAN));
00150 
00151         MAKENAME();
00152 
00153         flags = O_WRONLY|O_CREAT;
00154         if (stridesize == 1) {
00155                 flags |= O_TRUNC;
00156         }
00157 
00158         /* vfs_open destroys the string it's passed */
00159         strcpy(buf, name);
00160         err = vfs_open(buf, flags, 0664, &vn);
00161         if (err) {
00162                 kprintf("Could not open %s for write: %s\n", 
00163                         name, strerror(err));
00164                 return -1;
00165         }
00166 
00167         for (i=0; i<NCHUNKS; i++) {
00168                 if (i % stridesize != stridepos) {
00169                         pos += strlen(SLOGAN);
00170                         continue;
00171                 }
00172                 strcpy(buf, SLOGAN);
00173                 rotate(buf, i);
00174                 uio_kinit(&iov, &ku, buf, strlen(SLOGAN), pos, UIO_WRITE);
00175                 err = VOP_WRITE(vn, &ku);
00176                 if (err) {
00177                         kprintf("%s: Write error: %s\n", name, strerror(err));
00178                         vfs_close(vn);
00179                         vfs_remove(name);
00180                         return -1;
00181                 }
00182 
00183                 if (ku.uio_resid > 0) {
00184                         kprintf("%s: Short write: %lu bytes left over\n",
00185                                 name, (unsigned long) ku.uio_resid);
00186                         vfs_close(vn);
00187                         vfs_remove(name);
00188                         return -1;
00189                 }
00190 
00191                 bytes += (ku.uio_offset - pos);
00192                 shouldbytes += strlen(SLOGAN);
00193                 pos = ku.uio_offset;
00194         }
00195 
00196         vfs_close(vn);
00197 
00198         if (bytes != shouldbytes) {
00199                 kprintf("%s: %lu bytes written, should have been %lu!\n",
00200                         name, (unsigned long) bytes, 
00201                         (unsigned long) (NCHUNKS*strlen(SLOGAN)));
00202                 vfs_remove(name);
00203                 return -1;
00204         }
00205         kprintf("%s: %lu bytes written\n", name, (unsigned long) bytes);
00206 
00207         return 0;
00208 }
00209 
00210 static
00211 int
00212 fstest_read(const char *fs, const char *namesuffix)
00213 {
00214         struct vnode *vn;
00215         int err;
00216         int i;
00217         size_t bytes=0;
00218         char name[32];
00219         char buf[32];
00220         struct iovec iov;
00221         struct uio ku;
00222 
00223         MAKENAME();
00224 
00225         /* vfs_open destroys the string it's passed */
00226         strcpy(buf, name);
00227         err = vfs_open(buf, O_RDONLY, 0664, &vn);
00228         if (err) {
00229                 kprintf("Could not open test file for read: %s\n",
00230                         strerror(err));
00231                 return -1;
00232         }
00233 
00234         for (i=0; i<NCHUNKS; i++) {
00235                 uio_kinit(&iov, &ku, buf, strlen(SLOGAN), bytes, UIO_READ);
00236                 err = VOP_READ(vn, &ku);
00237                 if (err) {
00238                         kprintf("%s: Read error: %s\n", name, strerror(err));
00239                         vfs_close(vn);
00240                         return -1;
00241                 }
00242 
00243                 if (ku.uio_resid > 0) {
00244                         kprintf("%s: Short read: %lu bytes left over\n", name,
00245                                 (unsigned long) ku.uio_resid);
00246                         vfs_close(vn);
00247                         return -1;
00248                 }
00249                 buf[strlen(SLOGAN)] = 0;
00250                 rotate(buf, -i);
00251                 if (strcmp(buf, SLOGAN)) {
00252                         kprintf("%s: Test failed: line %d mismatched: %s\n",
00253                                 name, i+1, buf);
00254                         vfs_close(vn);
00255                         return -1;
00256                 }
00257 
00258                 bytes = ku.uio_offset;
00259         }
00260 
00261         vfs_close(vn);
00262 
00263         if (bytes != NCHUNKS*strlen(SLOGAN)) {
00264                 kprintf("%s: %lu bytes read, should have been %lu!\n",
00265                         name, (unsigned long) bytes, 
00266                         (unsigned long) (NCHUNKS*strlen(SLOGAN)));
00267                 return -1;
00268         }
00269         kprintf("%s: %lu bytes read\n", name, (unsigned long) bytes);
00270         return 0;
00271 }
00272 
00274 
00275 static
00276 void
00277 dofstest(const char *filesys)
00278 {
00279         kprintf("*** Starting filesystem test on %s:\n", filesys);
00280 
00281         if (fstest_write(filesys, "", 1, 0)) {
00282                 kprintf("*** Test failed\n");
00283                 return;
00284         }
00285         
00286         if (fstest_read(filesys, "")) {
00287                 kprintf("*** Test failed\n");
00288                 return;
00289         }
00290 
00291         if (fstest_remove(filesys, "")) {
00292                 kprintf("*** Test failed\n");
00293                 return;
00294         }
00295 
00296         kprintf("*** Filesystem test done\n");
00297 }
00298 
00300 
00301 static
00302 void
00303 readstress_thread(void *fs, unsigned long num)
00304 {
00305         const char *filesys = fs;
00306         if (fstest_read(filesys, "")) {
00307                 kprintf("*** Thread %lu: failed\n", num);
00308         }
00309         V(threadsem);
00310 }
00311 
00312 static
00313 void
00314 doreadstress(const char *filesys)
00315 {
00316         int i, err;
00317 
00318         init_threadsem();
00319 
00320         kprintf("*** Starting fs read stress test on %s:\n", filesys);
00321 
00322         if (fstest_write(filesys, "", 1, 0)) {
00323                 kprintf("*** Test failed\n");
00324                 return;
00325         }
00326 
00327         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00328                 err = thread_fork("readstress",
00329                                   readstress_thread, (char *)filesys, i, 
00330                                   NULL);
00331                 if (err) {
00332                         panic("readstress: thread_fork failed: %s\n",
00333                               strerror(err));
00334                 }
00335         }
00336 
00337         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00338                 P(threadsem);
00339         }
00340 
00341         if (fstest_remove(filesys, "")) {
00342                 kprintf("*** Test failed\n");
00343                 return;
00344         }
00345         
00346         kprintf("*** fs read stress test done\n");
00347 }
00348 
00350 
00351 static
00352 void
00353 writestress_thread(void *fs, unsigned long num)
00354 {
00355         const char *filesys = fs;
00356         char numstr[8];
00357         snprintf(numstr, sizeof(numstr), "%lu", num);
00358 
00359         if (fstest_write(filesys, numstr, 1, 0)) {
00360                 kprintf("*** Thread %lu: failed\n", num);
00361                 V(threadsem);
00362                 return;
00363         }
00364 
00365         if (fstest_read(filesys, numstr)) {
00366                 kprintf("*** Thread %lu: failed\n", num);
00367                 V(threadsem);
00368                 return;
00369         }
00370 
00371         if (fstest_remove(filesys, numstr)) {
00372                 kprintf("*** Thread %lu: failed\n", num);
00373         }
00374 
00375         kprintf("*** Thread %lu: done\n", num);
00376 
00377         V(threadsem);
00378 }
00379 
00380 static
00381 void
00382 dowritestress(const char *filesys)
00383 {
00384         int i, err;
00385 
00386         init_threadsem();
00387 
00388         kprintf("*** Starting fs write stress test on %s:\n", filesys);
00389 
00390         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00391                 err = thread_fork("writestress",
00392                                   writestress_thread, (char *)filesys, i, 
00393                                   NULL);
00394                 if (err) {
00395                         panic("thread_fork failed %s\n", strerror(err));
00396                 }
00397         }
00398 
00399         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00400                 P(threadsem);
00401         }
00402 
00403         kprintf("*** fs write stress test done\n");
00404 }
00405 
00407 
00408 static
00409 void
00410 writestress2_thread(void *fs, unsigned long num)
00411 {
00412         const char *filesys = fs;
00413 
00414         if (fstest_write(filesys, "", NTHREADS, num)) {
00415                 kprintf("*** Thread %lu: failed\n", num);
00416                 V(threadsem);
00417                 return;
00418         }
00419 
00420         V(threadsem);
00421 }
00422 
00423 static
00424 void
00425 dowritestress2(const char *filesys)
00426 {
00427         int i, err;
00428         char name[32];
00429         struct vnode *vn;
00430 
00431         init_threadsem();
00432 
00433         kprintf("*** Starting fs write stress test 2 on %s:\n", filesys);
00434 
00435         /* Create and truncate test file */
00436         fstest_makename(name, sizeof(name), filesys, "");
00437         err = vfs_open(name, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0664, &vn);
00438         if (err) {
00439                 kprintf("Could not create test file: %s\n", strerror(err));
00440                 kprintf("*** Test failed\n");
00441                 return;
00442         }
00443         vfs_close(vn);
00444 
00445         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00446                 err = thread_fork("writestress2",
00447                                   writestress2_thread, (char *)filesys, i, 
00448                                   NULL);
00449                 if (err) {
00450                         panic("writestress2: thread_fork failed: %s\n",
00451                               strerror(err));
00452                 }
00453         }
00454 
00455         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00456                 P(threadsem);
00457         }
00458 
00459         if (fstest_read(filesys, "")) {
00460                 kprintf("*** Test failed\n");
00461                 return;
00462         }
00463 
00464         if (fstest_remove(filesys, "")) {
00465                 kprintf("*** Test failed\n");
00466         }
00467 
00468 
00469         kprintf("*** fs write stress test 2 done\n");
00470 }
00471 
00473 
00474 static
00475 void
00476 createstress_thread(void *fs, unsigned long num)
00477 {
00478         const char *filesys = fs;
00479         int i;
00480         char numstr[16];
00481 
00482         for (i=0; i<NCREATES; i++) {
00483 
00484                 snprintf(numstr, sizeof(numstr), "%lu-%d", num, i);
00485 
00486                 if (fstest_write(filesys, numstr, 1, 0)) {
00487                         kprintf("*** Thread %lu: file %d: failed\n", num, i);
00488                         V(threadsem);
00489                         return;
00490                 }
00491                 
00492                 if (fstest_read(filesys, numstr)) {
00493                         kprintf("*** Thread %lu: file %d: failed\n", num, i);
00494                         V(threadsem);
00495                         return;
00496                 }
00497 
00498                 if (fstest_remove(filesys, numstr)) {
00499                         kprintf("*** Thread %lu: file %d: failed\n", num, i);
00500                         V(threadsem);
00501                         return;
00502                 }
00503 
00504         }
00505 
00506         V(threadsem);
00507 }
00508 
00509 static
00510 void
00511 docreatestress(const char *filesys)
00512 {
00513         int i, err;
00514 
00515         init_threadsem();
00516 
00517         kprintf("*** Starting fs create stress test on %s:\n", filesys);
00518 
00519         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00520                 err = thread_fork("createstress",
00521                                   createstress_thread, (char *)filesys, i, 
00522                                   NULL);
00523                 if (err) {
00524                         panic("createstress: thread_fork failed %s\n",
00525                               strerror(err));
00526                 }
00527         }
00528 
00529         for (i=0; i<NTHREADS; i++) {
00530                 P(threadsem);
00531         }
00532 
00533         kprintf("*** fs create stress test done\n");
00534 }
00535 
00537 
00538 static
00539 int
00540 checkfilesystem(int nargs, char **args)
00541 {
00542         char *device;
00543 
00544         if (nargs != 2) {
00545                 kprintf("Usage: fs[12345] filesystem:\n");
00546                 return EINVAL;
00547         }
00548 
00549         device = args[1];
00550 
00551         /* Allow (but do not require) colon after device name */
00552         if (device[strlen(device)-1]==':') {
00553                 device[strlen(device)-1] = 0;
00554         }
00555 
00556         return 0;
00557 }
00558 
00559 #define DEFTEST(testname)                         \
00560   int                                             \
00561   testname(int nargs, char **args)                \
00562   {                                               \
00563         int result;                               \
00564         result = checkfilesystem(nargs, args);    \
00565         if (result) {                             \
00566                 return result;                    \
00567         }                                         \
00568         do##testname(args[1]);                    \
00569         return 0;                                 \
00570   }
00571 
00572 DEFTEST(fstest);
00573 DEFTEST(readstress);
00574 DEFTEST(writestress);
00575 DEFTEST(writestress2);
00576 DEFTEST(createstress);
00577 
00579 
00580 int
00581 printfile(int nargs, char **args)
00582 {
00583         struct vnode *rv, *wv;
00584         struct iovec iov;
00585         struct uio ku;
00586         off_t rpos=0, wpos=0;
00587         char buf[128];
00588         char outfile[16];
00589         int result;
00590         int done=0;
00591 
00592         if (nargs != 2) {
00593                 kprintf("Usage: pf filename\n");
00594                 return EINVAL;
00595         }
00596 
00597         /* vfs_open destroys the string it's passed; make a copy */
00598         strcpy(outfile, "con:");
00599 
00600         result = vfs_open(args[1], O_RDONLY, 0664, &rv);
00601         if (result) {
00602                 kprintf("printfile: %s\n", strerror(result));
00603                 return result;
00604         }
00605 
00606         result = vfs_open(outfile, O_WRONLY, 0664, &wv);
00607         if (result) {
00608                 kprintf("printfile: output: %s\n", strerror(result));
00609                 vfs_close(rv);
00610                 return result;
00611         }
00612 
00613         while (!done) {
00614                 uio_kinit(&iov, &ku, buf, sizeof(buf), rpos, UIO_READ);
00615                 result = VOP_READ(rv, &ku);
00616                 if (result) {
00617                         kprintf("Read error: %s\n", strerror(result));
00618                         break;
00619                 }
00620                 rpos = ku.uio_offset;
00621 
00622                 if (ku.uio_resid > 0) {
00623                         done = 1;
00624                 }
00625 
00626                 uio_kinit(&iov, &ku, buf, sizeof(buf)-ku.uio_resid, wpos,
00627                           UIO_WRITE);
00628                 result = VOP_WRITE(wv, &ku);
00629                 if (result) {
00630                         kprintf("Write error: %s\n", strerror(result));
00631                         break;
00632                 }
00633                 wpos = ku.uio_offset;
00634 
00635                 if (ku.uio_resid > 0) {
00636                         kprintf("Warning: short write\n");
00637                 }
00638         }
00639 
00640         vfs_close(wv);
00641         vfs_close(rv);
00642 
00643         return 0;
00644 }
Generated on Sun Jun 27 22:12:13 2010 for Os/161 by  1.6.1
